skill.name: happyhorse-1-0
skill.description: Generate text-to-video with HappyHorse 1.0 on RunComfy. Documents HappyHorse 1.0's strengths (#1 on Artificial Analysis Video Arena, native 1080p with in-pass synchronized audio, multi-shot character consistency, 6-language prompt support), the duration / aspect-ratio / resolution schema, and when to route to Wan 2.7 / Seedance 2 / LTX 2 instead. Calls `runcomfy run happyhorse/happyhorse-1-0/text-to-video` through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "happyhorse", "happy horse", "happyhorse 1.0", "happyhorse video", or any explicit ask to generate video with this model.
skill.summary: Generate professional 1080p videos with native audio using HappyHorse 1.0, the top-rated text-to-video model on Artificial Analysis Video Arena.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 86 potential issues, but evaluation confirms all are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill is a legitimate text-to-video wrapper that passes user prompts as JSON-encoded strings via the RunComfy CLI, not shell arguments. Network access is limited to the RunComfy API endpoint only. Token storage uses proper file permissions (0600). No malicious code patterns, shell injection vectors, or prompt injection attempts detected.

risk_factor_evidence.notes: Shell command invokes the RunComfy CLI with hardcoded arguments. User prompts are passed as JSON strings via --input flag, not shell-expanded.
Only communicates with model-api.runcomfy.net and *.runcomfy.net/*.runcomfy.com for API calls and output downloads. No telemetry or external callbacks.

content.value_statement: Creating video content requires expensive software and technical expertise. HappyHorse 1.0 generates high-quality 1080p videos with synchronized audio from text prompts, making professional video creation accessible to anyone with a RunComfy account.
actual_capabilities: Generate 1080p videos up to 15 seconds from text prompts in 6 languages (CN/EN/JP/KR/DE/FR)
Create multi-shot video sequences with consistent character appearance across shots
Produce videos with synchronized audio (dialogue, ambient sound, Foley) in the same generation pass
Generate vertical, horizontal, and square videos for different platforms
Access the top-rated text-to-video model according to Artificial Analysis Video Arena
Control output quality (720P/1080P) and seed values for reproducible results
limitations: Video duration limited to 15 seconds maximum; longer narratives require multi-shot stitching
Only 5 aspect ratios supported (16:9, 9:16, 1:1, 4:3, 3:4); ultra-wide formats get cropped
Cannot pass external audio files for lip-sync; audio must be generated in-pass
Requires a RunComfy account and CLI installation; not a standalone solution

use_cases.title: Multi-Shot Brand Storytelling
use_cases.description: Marketing teams create consistent character-driven video stories across multiple shots. Describe the anchor character once, reuse traits across shots, and produce a cohesive brand narrative.

use_cases.title: Cinematic Conceptualization
use_cases.description: Filmmakers and creative directors generate visual references for pre-production. Test camera angles, lighting directions, and motion descriptions before committing to expensive shoots.

use_cases.title: Multilingual Product Ads
use_cases.description: E-commerce teams produce platform-native video ads in multiple languages without re-shooting. Write prompts in any supported language for localization.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Video Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a video of [describe your scene, action, and camera setup].
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a simple video from a text description

prompt_templates.title: Cinematic Shot with Camera Direction
prompt_templates.prompt: [Shot type, e.g., Wide shot/Mid-shot]. [Describe main subject with details]. [Describe motion/action over time]. [Specify lens/lighting feel, e.g., shallow DOF, crushed shadows].
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want professional camera direction in your video

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Shot Consistency
prompt_templates.prompt: Shot 1: [Describe scene with full character/setting anchor]. Shot 2: [Same character with changed detail], [describe new action or location].
prompt_templates.scenario: When creating a narrative sequence with consistent characters

prompt_templates.title: Vertical Platform Video
prompt_templates.prompt: 9:16 vertical short. [Describe scene in close-up/medium shot]. [Detail any foreground elements, e.g., steam, rain]. [Specify audio direction if needed].
prompt_templates.scenario: When generating content optimized for TikTok, Reels, or Shorts

output_examples.input: A barista in a black apron pulls a single espresso shot, steam rising into the morning sun
output_examples.output: A smooth video showing the espresso extraction process with warm lighting, rising steam, and the rich crema forming slowly. The handheld close-up camera with shallow depth of field captures the morning cafe atmosphere.

output_examples.input: Shot 1: Woman in navy trench enters a Tokyo alley. Shot 2: Same woman under a ramen shop awning
output_examples.output: Two linked clips featuring a consistent character in a navy coat across different locations. The second shot shows her shaking water off an umbrella with warm interior glow and gentle rain sounds.

output_examples.input: Wide shot astronaut skiing on the moon with parallel tracks in gray regolith
output_examples.output: A cinematic landscape video of an astronaut in dusty orange suit with blue-gray harness on a lunar plain. The 1/6th gravity drift and dust haze create a realistic sci-fi atmosphere against the Earth on the horizon.

best_practices: Use temporal verbs that describe motion over time rather than static states; say 'turns and walks' not just 'stands'
Front-load camera and shot direction in your prompt; 'Wide shot' or 'Tracking shot' works as a real directive
Restate character anchors in each shot when using multi-shot consistency; HappyHorse holds the look but needs the anchor repeated
anti_patterns: Static-frame descriptions with no temporal verbs produce vague, generic motion
Conflicting style directions (dark + bright, warm + cool) cancel each other out
Prompts over 2,500 characters degrade quality; keep descriptions focused on one visual beat per clip

faq.question: What is HappyHorse 1.0 and why should I use it?
faq.answer: HappyHorse 1.0 is a text-to-video AI model ranked #1 on the Artificial Analysis Video Arena. It produces native 1080p video with synchronized audio and excels at multi-shot character consistency across longer narratives.

faq.question: Do I need a RunComfy account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You need to install the RunComfy CLI with 'npm i -g @runcomfy/cli', create an account at runcomfy.com, and run 'runcomfy login'. For CI environments, set the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable.

faq.question: What video formats and durations does it support?
faq.answer: HappyHorse 1.0 supports 16:9, 9:16, 1:1, 4:3, and 3:4 aspect ratios with resolutions of 720P or 1080P. Outputs are 3-15 seconds long.

faq.question: Can I add my own audio files for lip-sync?
faq.answer: No. HappyHorse 1.0 generates audio synchronously within the same pass. For audio-driven lip-sync with reference video, use Seedance 2.0 Pro instead.

faq.question: How do I create consistent characters across multiple shots?
faq.answer: Restate the character anchor description in each shot. For example: 'Shot 1: woman in red coat. Shot 2: same woman in red coat, now walking through a door.' HappyHorse maintains appearance based on anchor mentions.

faq.question: What happens if my prompt is too long?
faq.answer: Prompts over 2,500 characters may degrade video quality. Keep descriptions focused on one visual beat per clip. Use multi-shot prompts for longer narratives instead of cramming everything into one prompt.
